perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will commence upon the Effective Date and terminate on the expiry or termination of any engagement in relation to the Subject Matter between the Parties. Upon termination or expiry of this Agreement, the Recipient (i) will cease to use the C onfidential Information received from the Discloser, (ii) within thirty (30) days after the date of termination or expiry of this Agreement, will return to the Discloser all original Confidential Information thereof then in its possession or control, (iii) if the Discloser requests in writing, within thirty (30) days after the date of termination or expiry of this Agreement, will return to the Discloser all copies of the Confidential Information requested by the Discloser, (iv) if no request from the Disclo ser has been received within thirty (30) days of the termination or expiry of this agreement for the return of any copies of the Confidential Information, will destroy all copies thereof then in its possession or control and (v) upon the written request o f the Discloser, will furnish the Discloser with written certification of such destruction .
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will commence upon the Effective Date and terminate on the expiry or termination of any engagement in relation to the Subject Matter between the Parties. Upon termination or expiry of this Agreement, the Recipient (i) will cease to use the C onfidential Information received from the Discloser.
perturbation.explanation: By removing clauses (ii) through (v) in Section 10.1, this creates an in-text contradiction. While the Recipient is obligated to cease using Confidential Information upon termination as stated in (i), the agreement now lacks specific instructions regarding the return or destruction of that information. This omission directly contradicts the original intention of securely managing Confidential Information upon termination, as stated in the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement will commence upon the Effective Date and terminate on the expiry or termination of any engagement in relation to the Subject Matter between the Parties. Upon termination or expiry of this Agreement, the Recipient (i) will cease to use the C onfidential Information received from the Discloser.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes clauses (ii) through (v) from the original text which specified the Recipient's obligations regarding the return or destruction of Confidential Information upon termination. This removal directly contradicts the implied intention of the agreement to securely manage Confidential Information upon termination, creating an in-text contradiction as the contradicted text only obligates the recipient to cease using the information. Therefore, the modified text creates a direct conflict and inconsistency within the document, satisfying the definition of Omissions - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Recipient agrees that, in the event of any breach of any provision hereof, the Discloser may, or will, not have an adequate remedy in money or damages. The Recipient therefore agrees that, in such event, the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against such breach in any court of competent jurisdiction. Such injunctive relief will in no way limit the Discloser’s right to obtain other remedies available at law or in equity. No failure or delay by the Discloser hereto i n enforcing any right, power or privilege created hereunder shall operate as an implied waiver thereof, nor shall any single or partial enforcement thereof preclude any other or further enforcement thereof or the enforcement of any other right, power or pr ivilege.
perturbation.changed_text: The Recipient agrees that, in the event of any breach of any provision hereof, the Discloser may, or will, not have an adequate remedy in money or damages. The Recipient therefore agrees that, in such event, the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against such breach in any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the sentence "Such injunctive relief will in no way limit the Discloser’s right to obtain other remedies available at law or in equity." this introduces an omission. Now this section implies injunctive relief is the only recourse available to the Discloser. By not stating the Discloser is able to seek other remedies, it can create uncertainty and limit the Discloser ability to be fully compensated. This can create ambiguity in section 5 about whether other remedies can be pursue.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient agrees that, in the event of any breach of any provision hereof, the Discloser may, or will, not have an adequate remedy in money or damages. The Recipient therefore agrees that, in such event, the Discloser shall be entitled to obtain injunctive relief against such breach in any court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that injunctive relief does not limit the Discloser's right to other remedies. The changed text removes this statement, implying that injunctive relief might be the sole remedy available, which contradicts the original intent and could create uncertainty regarding other remedies, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. The Contradicted Text and Changed text are effectively the same as the Changed text is a truncated version of the Contradicted text. However, based on the Perturbation Explanation, the change has caused a contradiction due to omission.
